slides.title: Snoring can be a *warning sign* for your heart.
slides.sub: Untreated sleep apnea quietly strains the body every night. Here is why screening matters.

slides.title: Breathing pauses in the night
slides.body: Obstructive sleep apnea is when the airway repeatedly collapses in sleep. Loud snoring and gasping *can be early clues*.

slides.title: Oxygen dips, again and again
slides.body: Each pause drops blood oxygen, called intermittent hypoxia. The body jolts awake to breathe, *often without you knowing*.

slides.title: Pressure on the heart
slides.body: Those repeated dips spike blood pressure and stress the heart. Untreated apnea *is linked to* hypertension and heart disease.

slides.title: Sharpness pays the price
slides.body: Fragmented, low-oxygen sleep can dull memory and focus over time. Treating apnea *may help* protect long-term brain health.

slides.title: It is highly treatable
slides.body: A simple screening can lead to therapy that restores steady breathing. Many people *feel sharper within weeks* of treatment.

slides.title: Make your sleep *work for you*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep, food, movement and steps, then turns them into one daily Health Score so you can spot what your nights are telling you.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
That nightly snoring might be more than noise. Untreated sleep apnea drops your oxygen over and over, and your heart pays for it. 😴🫀

Repeated oxygen dips can spike blood pressure and strain the heart and brain, but screening and treatment can turn it around. Save this for later. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your sleep and daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
